history.question: Should the map include visible zoom controls (like + and − buttons) on the screen, or should zooming only be available through mouse scroll wheel and pinch gestures on touch devices?

However, I am not sure about the specific details regarding whether visible zoom controls (like + and − buttons) should be included on screen, or if zooming should only be available through mouse scroll and pinch gestures. If visible zoom controls are implemented, they would fall under UI pieces and should be styled hotpink according to my preference. Could you ask a more specific question about other map interactions (such as dragging and marking locations), keyword search functionality, or visual styling preferences?

history.question: When a user types a keyword into the search bar and submits the search, should the map automatically zoom and center on the first matching result, or should it stay at the current zoom level and just highlight all matching locations within the visible area?

history.question: When a user enters a keyword search, should the search accept only address and place-name queries, or should it also support other input types like coordinates (latitude/longitude) or category searches (e.g., 'coffee shops', 'gas stations')?
history.answer: I am not sure about the specific types of input the keyword search should accept at this time. Could you ask a more specific question about the basic map interaction (such as zooming, dragging, and marking locations), keyword search functionality, or visual styling preferences?

history.question: Should users be able to drag the map to pan around and explore different areas, or should the map view be locked to only show the area around the searched or marked location?
history.answer: Yes, users should be able to drag the map to pan around and explore different areas. I expect dragging to be one of the core map interactions, along with zooming and marking locations.

history.question: When a user places a pin or marker on the map, should a small label or popup appear next to it showing the address or coordinates of that location, or should the marker appear silently without any attached information?
